result.summary: mediawiki.org documents two HTTP developer surfaces on its own host: the MediaWiki Action API at `/w/api.php` and the MediaWiki REST API at `/w/rest.php`; docs also describe OAuth consumer registration and bot passwords, but the public docs reviewed do not cleanly bind those credentials to these specific mediawiki.org endpoints.
result.description: MediaWiki is open-source wiki software used to run collaborative websites such as wikis. The mediawiki.org site hosts documentation, code-related resources, and a running MediaWiki instance exposing its own APIs.

mw_oauth_client.label: MediaWiki OAuth client registration
mw_oauth_client.setup: Log in to the target wiki and register an OAuth consumer via [Special:OAuthConsumerRegistration/propose](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Special:OAuthConsumerRegistration/propose). The [OAuth developer guide](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/OAuth/For_Developers) explains registration and notes MediaWiki supports both OAuth `1.0a` and OAuth `2.0` for wikis running Extension:OAuth. Use the issued client credentials/tokens with API requests on behalf of a user.

mw_bot_password.label: MediaWiki bot password
mw_bot_password.setup: Create a bot/app password at [Special:BotPasswords](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Special:BotPasswords). The [bot passwords manual](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Manual:Bot_passwords) says bot passwords let applications log in with a simple username/password combination for API access only, with limited grants. Use your bot username plus the generated bot password when authenticating through the API login flow.
